name: Investigate Noise
description: An AI behavior where an agent reacts to an auditory or disturbance event by moving to inspect its source or last known position.
design_notes: Noise investigation bridges stealth, patrol, and chase. It needs perception_event metadata, source confidence, last_known_position, investigation radius, timeout, and resume rules so distractions feel useful but not magical.
design_purpose: Let players manipulate AI attention with footsteps, thrown objects, alarms, or decoys.
Create a readable middle state between calm patrol and full chase.
Make spaces feel responsive without requiring every stimulus to become combat.
player_fantasy: The player feels clever when they lure an enemy away, slip past during the search, or bait attention into a trap.

parameters.name: last_known_position
parameters.description: Validated source position the agent will inspect or search around.

parameters.name: noise_loudness_radius
parameters.description: Range within which AI can receive the stimulus before occlusion or hearing modifiers.

parameters.name: search_sweep_commitment_ms
parameters.description: Search commitment window before resuming patrol or escalating.

parameters.name: source_confidence_threshold
parameters.description: Minimum confidence needed to leave current behavior for the noise.

edge_cases: Several noise events arrive in one decision interval and need deterministic priority ordering.
A noise source is unreachable, inside a wall, or outside the agent's allowed zone.
The agent hears a noise while already chasing a target and must compare threat_score against curiosity.
A decoy is picked up or destroyed before the agent reaches last_known_position.
common_bugs: Investigation uses the sound emitter transform after it has moved instead of the captured event position.
AI forgets the original patrol_route_id and cannot resume after investigation_duration_ms.
Every nearby agent responds identically because squad_id coordination or reservation is missing.
Noise occlusion is ignored, so enemies hear quiet events through thick walls.
balancing_notes: Noise tools should be reliable enough for planning but limited by line, distance, cooldown, or enemy suspicion state.
If investigation always escalates to chase, players stop using distractions as stealth tools.
Group callouts should create pressure without pulling an entire level from one minor sound.
accessibility_notes: Show or teach which player actions make noise, using visual indicators as well as sound.
For players with hearing loss, provide readable suspicion markers or minimap pulses for caused distractions.

implementation_notes.unity: Publish sound events with perception_event_id, position, loudness, and source actor; AI stores last_known_position and debug_reason_trace before issuing a NavMesh query.
implementation_notes.godot: Route noise through a signal/event bus and evaluate hearing radius plus occlusion before setting blackboard-like state on agents; draw investigation circles in debug builds.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use AI Perception hearing stimuli or custom gameplay messages; Blackboard keys should include NoiseLocation, NoiseTag, Confidence, and ResumeState for Behavior Tree abort rules.
implementation_notes.web: Store sound events in a deterministic queue sorted by timestamp and source ID; grid or nav agents should validate reachability before switching to investigate state.

relationships.reason: Noise investigation needs loudness, radius, surface, and occlusion data from the stealth noise model.
Distraction objects create noise events that investigation behavior can prioritize.
Investigation interrupts patrol routes and creates a recoverable search state before returning to normal behavior.

example_games.title: The Last of Us
example_games.note: Uses thrown objects and sound to redirect enemies.

example_games.title: Hitman
example_games.note: Uses distractions to move NPCs and guards.
